name: Statement Ingestion
description: RECOMMENDED APPROACH A — STAGED LOCAL ADAPTER PIPELINE. Treat ingestion as a provenance-and-commit protocol, not as a universal document parser. A versioned source adapter for each evidenced bank format produces an immutable candidate batch and reconciliation manifest without mutating LEDGER. INGEST validates account mapping, exact amounts, currency, source dates, source references, source fingerprint and row identity, then exposes an explicit preview. Commit invokes only the discoverable versioned LEDGER contract with stable idempotency identities and emits a resumable receipt covering every candidate. Start with the smallest real formats needed by the three accounts; an adapter seam permits later formats without building a generic extraction platform. Complexity M for evidenced CSV, Excel, and text-native layouts; it exceeds the budget if it turns into generic OCR. Root-problem completeness 9/10. It builds directly on FR-LEDGER-CONTRACT-DISCOVERY, FR-LEDGER-STRUCTURED-INVOCATION, FR-LEDGER-TRANSACTION-RECORDING, and FR-LEDGER-IDEMPOTENT-WRITES. Biggest risk: a parser can produce financially plausible but semantically wrong rows. Stress test: if representative fixtures cannot reconcile every source row and required field deterministically, that format remains unsupported and the do-less path wins. ALTERNATIVE B — DIRECT STREAMING IMPORT. Parse a file and immediately invoke LEDGER once per row, relying on idempotency for retry. Complexity M and root completeness 6/10. It reduces staging work, but parsing errors and partial commits are visible only after durable mutation, review is weak, and recovery semantics become harder to explain. Rejected because the safety loss attacks the root problem. Biggest risk: a partially committed or misparsed batch looks complete. ALTERNATIVE C — DO LESS WITH NORMALIZED CSV. Require bank-provided CSV or an owner-prepared canonical CSV and implement only validation plus commit. Complexity L, root completeness 5/10, and strongly within budget. This is an honest first-format or fallback when all initial accounts export reliable machine-readable data; it loses direct PDF and Excel support and preserves manual conversion work. Biggest risk: manual normalization recreates the omissions and semantic drift INGEST exists to prevent. SELECTION. Choose Approach A, but constrain it to evidenced adapters and allow Approach C to replace any format that cannot be parsed locally and reconciled deterministically. The non-obvious inversion is that parsers are disposable boundary adapters; the durable product is the manifest, provenance, preview, idempotency, and receipt contract. SCOPE SIGNALS AND ROUTING. Signals present: sensitive financial-data security, external bank-file formats, a cross-system flow into LEDGER, a likely import model exceeding five entities, and significant unknowns around PDF extraction, locale, sign, date, duplicate identity, reconciliation, and source retention. Five signals plus security and major unknowns route next to /lex:discovery INGEST. A requirement for a new cross-module LEDGER batch contract or a project-wide source-retention policy should be considered for /lex:adr after discovery evidence; this brainstorm does not decide either.

DISCOVERY 0.2 — EVIDENCE LIMITATION. No bank extract was present in the workspace for this discovery run. The protocol, safety rules, workflows, and adapter qualification outcomes are discoverable now, but no bank, extension, layout, field mapping, locale, row identity, or reconciliation control is claimed as supported. OQ-INGEST-1 through OQ-INGEST-5 are explicit pre-planning fixture-spike gates; they do not block PRD authoring because the PRD can require qualification without promising a format.

ACTORS AND CURRENT STATE. P1 Personal Finance Owner is reused as the sole human actor. The AI Agent Host is a supporting automated client, not a persona. Today P1 downloads separate statements, manually reads or converts them, transcribes selected facts, infers sign/date/account meaning, removes suspected duplicates or transfers, and recalculates totals. The pain is slow recurring work, incomplete coverage, unreproducible conversion choices, weak duplicate confidence, and no durable proof that every source transaction reached LEDGER.

DESIRED WORKFLOWS AND GAPS. UC-INGEST-001 passively detects, extracts, validates, reconciles, and previews one local statement without mutation. UC-INGEST-002 reviews the exact immutable manifest and requires explicit approval without editing source financial facts. UC-INGEST-003 commits candidates through public idempotent LEDGER writes and resumes interruptions from a durable receipt. UC-INGEST-004 distinguishes exact replay/duplicates from blocking overlap conflicts. UC-INGEST-005 reports and cleans up failed or abandoned ingestion safely. UC-INGEST-006 lets P1 or an AI Agent Host discover and invoke the complete machine contract. CLASSIFY is an explicit separate post-commit command, not an INGEST side effect.

PRE-MORTEM. Six months after launch, failure is most likely if (1) a bank layout drifts and a plausible but wrong amount, sign, date, account, or row mapping reaches LEDGER — RISK-INGEST-001/002; (2) replay, overlap, corrected statements, or a crash creates duplicates or an unknowable partial batch — RISK-INGEST-003/005; or (3) source, temporary, manifest, or receipt data leaks or malformed active content exhausts the host — RISK-INGEST-004/007. These risks drive the deep areas and the fixture, frozen-manifest, exact-only duplicate, passive-parsing, owner-only, and resume rules.
DOMAIN AREA DISPOSITIONS.
- auth/authz — CHECK: the sole-owner local process trusts the invoking OS identity; any daemon, remote client, UI, or multi-user mode reopens authentication/authorization and is outside INGEST.

- data model and persistence — DEEP: immutable manifest revisions, candidate outcomes, durable receipts, retention, exact replay, overlap conflicts, idempotency, and crash resume are central to G1/G3/G4/G5 and RISK-INGEST-003/005.
- UI and navigation — N/A: NG8 assigns screens and five-state presentation to CHANNELS; INGEST exposes a structured CLI contract only.
- integrations — DEEP: owner-supplied statement data, AI Agent Host invocation, public LEDGER writes, version compatibility, and the explicit post-commit CLASSIFY boundary determine the workflow.
- background and async — N/A: NG7 forbids watchers, schedulers, and a required daemon; every import is explicitly invoked.

- reporting and analytics — CHECK: INGEST reports extraction/reconciliation evidence and terminal counts only; budgets, trends, narrative insight, and ongoing account reconciliation remain BUDGET/INSIGHTS or NG6.
- notifications — N/A: no INGEST goal sends notifications; any delivery surface belongs to CHANNELS.

- configuration and admin — CHECK: supported adapters, safe limits, retained batch status, abandon, and cleanup are discoverable operations; no bank credential, live connector, or arbitrary parser configuration is introduced.
- multi-tenancy and isolation — N/A: the accepted product boundary is one Personal Finance Owner under one OS identity; multi-person or hosted isolation is outside scope.

- source format and financial semantics — DEEP: exact record boundaries, locale, account mapping, amount sign, dates, source identity, passive parsing, and source controls determine whether G1/G2/G3/G6 are trustworthy.

- local security and privacy — DEEP: sensitive source/temp/manifest/receipt artifacts, passive bounded processing, owner-only permissions, metadata-only diagnostics, source preservation, and crash cleanup drive C3/C11/C12 and RISK-INGEST-004/007.

INTEGRATED DOMAIN RULES. A supported Format Variant is fixture-proven, explicitly detectable, passive, bounded, and fail-closed; extension matching is insufficient. P1 supplies one active canonical account identity and statement metadata validates it. Every source record is accounted for explicitly. A transaction-bearing record must be accepted, proven an Exact Duplicate, or blocked; any rejected or ambiguous transaction-bearing record keeps the batch uncommittable, while non-transaction records may be excluded with a stable reason. Source-derived financial facts cannot be edited in INGEST. Preview freezes a Reviewed Manifest Revision; any decision change creates a new preview. Commit never rereads the source and may progress candidate by candidate only with stable LEDGER identities and a durable outcome before continuation. Exact Replay is safe; only identity-grade matches are automatic duplicates; similarity produces an Overlap Conflict. Resume reuses the same manifest, request, versions, and identities. The source stays caller-owned and unchanged.

SECURITY AND COMPLIANCE. Selective threat analysis covers disclosure, source/manifest tampering, outcome repudiation, and parser denial/resource exhaustion. Owner-only storage, no payload logging or process arguments, frozen fingerprints/manifests, attributable receipts, passive content handling, safe resource bounds, source preservation, and deterministic cleanup address them. Spoofing/elevation are bounded by the accepted OS-identity trust boundary. Analogous LEDGER discovery found the current purely personal/household local scope outside POPIA processing obligations; cloud parsing, hosting, multi-person access, or third-party processing requires reassessment and currently violates a boundary or kill criterion.

FEASIBILITY. G1 known/high once a variant is qualified; G2 known/high through the accepted LEDGER transaction contract; G3 complex/high through full row accounting and immutable review; G4 known/medium-high through accepted LEDGER discovery/structured/idempotent contracts, with runtime conformance still unbuilt; G5 complex/medium because exact replay is known but variant row identity remains A3; G6 uncertain/low because no extract exists, covered by A1/A2/A3, RISK-INGEST-001/002/006, and OQ-INGEST-1 through OQ-INGEST-5 recommending /lex:spike; G7 known/high by analogy to the validated LEDGER Agent Host contract. Low confidence is therefore explicit and does not harden a format claim in PRD.

ASSUMPTION BASELINE FOR PRD. A1 assumes at least one bounded local export path per initial account; A2 assumes one account per supported artifact; A3 assumes a deterministic row identity per adopted variant; A4 supplies a conservative minimal-retention default. Each remains visible and owner-revisable. OQ-INGEST-6 through OQ-INGEST-10 are resolved to candidate-granular resume, minimal retention, explicit post-commit classification, explicit account selection, and stable safe diagnostic categories.

SCOPE NOTE. Discovery operationalizes existing goals through six use cases, six additional constraints, four assumptions, dependency gates, security risk RISK-INGEST-007, metrics, and precise terminology. It does not add live banking, category selection, analytics, generic OCR, private LEDGER access, account reconciliation, background automation, or UI.

KILL-CRITERIA STATUS. No criterion is triggered. Machine-readable-only coverage and per-format reconciliation cannot yet be evaluated because no fixtures exist, so no PDF/XLSX or other adapter is promised and the normalized CSV do-less option remains live. The selected protocol requires no cloud upload, bank credentials, custom cryptography, or generic OCR. The accepted LEDGER contract supplies the required provenance and idempotent per-candidate writes without private coupling. Fixture scope remains bounded to the initial accounts. Recheck all five criteria after the pre-planning fixture spike and again before design/planning.
problem_statement: Manual entry cannot keep the three-account Financial Ledger complete, while two banks provide heterogeneous PDF, CSV, and Excel statements whose account, amount-sign, date, reference, and row-identity conventions are not yet verified. The 5 Whys chain is: manual entry is too slow for historical and recurring data; the files contain the needed scale; each format and bank encodes the facts differently; ad hoc parsing can silently omit, duplicate, or misstate plausible-looking rows; and every later classification, budget, and insight becomes untrustworthy when the canonical facts are wrong. The root problem is the absence of a deterministic local statement-to-LEDGER import boundary that preserves source provenance, exposes ambiguity before mutation, and makes retries duplicate-safe. The desired journey is agent-first: the owner downloads a statement, an agent discovers INGEST operations, selects a supported adapter, previews candidate transactions and reconciliation evidence, resolves blocking ambiguity, explicitly commits accepted candidates through public LEDGER commands, and receives a receipt proving every accepted, duplicate, rejected, and failed outcome. Without this, LEDGER remains incomplete or populated through unauditable manual work.
why_now: The LEDGER contract has passed documentation review, but its OQ-LEDGER-1 through OQ-LEDGER-4 evidence gates and later real use require representative bank files. Framing INGEST now validates the first downstream producer contract before LEDGER beads are compiled and creates the safe path for historical and recurring population once the core commands exist.

rationale: Imports may request automatic category suggestions, but uncategorized transactions remain valid.
Every accepted statement row must become or match a canonical ledger transaction.
